author: 3d114fcc84b1ea9b2203886d0381fc69b5f8cfb327ca3f29
body: Let me respond to a few of the standard types of comments we see for weather-related news stories: 1. "Climate change!", "Global warming!"..Response: No, it's a natural weather event. Storms happen every year. Floods happen every year. Even if they are "one in a hundred year" events for the area affected..remember that the earth is 4.5 billion years old. So there's been literally millions of "one in a hundred year" events. Climate change is a natural process that's been occurring for billions of years. While human activity is a factor and we need to be far more responsible, there's many other causes of climate change that are completely beyond our control. 2. "These are RECORD weather events!"...Response: Remember, we only have accurate weather data for about 100 years. And we only have accurate data for storm strength (tornadoes, cyclones, hurricanes etc) for about 50 years. On a 4.5 billion year old planet. The word "record" is very misleading. 3. "This storm is a sign from God. Repent!" ...Response: Aside from the fact one must always say "a God" (there's been thousands of myths), bringing up fictional characters and quoting fictional stories like the bible for natural events is illogical. It's EXACTLY like blaming the storm on a Pink Unicorn in the sky or quoting a Harry Potter book. This saves me a lot of time responding to these type of comments in this section.

body: While appearing to the children of men as a great physician who can heal all their maladies, he will bring disease and disaster, until populous cities are reduced to ruin and desolation. Even now he is at work. In accidents and calamities by sea and by land, in great conflagrations, in fierce tornadoes and terrific hailstorms, in tempests, floods, cyclones, tidal waves, and earthquakes, in every place and in a thousand forms, Satan is exercising his power. He sweeps away the ripening harvest, and famine and distress follow. He imparts to the air a deadly taint, and thousands perish by the pestilence. These visitations are to become more and more frequent and disastrous. Destruction will be upon both man and beast. "The earth mourneth and fadeth away," "the haughty people . . .do languish. The earth also is defiled under the inhabitants thereof; because they have transgressed the laws, changed the ordinance, broken the everlasting covenant." (Isaiah 24:4, 5).

author: c34522c84b6d6e49fda6a9620d75cadc5b5cf6a10ea6d963
body: All the places where I grew up. Mozambique has always had extreme weather, but now these mega storms devastate the little that was left of this poor, struggling, beautiful country with beautiful people. Estudei na escola da Manga, e em Mafambisse. Vivi em varios locais, incluindo a Munhava, Manga, Beira, Mafambisse, e iamos muitas vezes a praia do Macuti. Passamos muito pelo Dondo, Vila Pery, Vila Manica, Gondola, Dombe, e Sunssundenga. Vivemos tambem num acampamento do Buzi, mesmo ao pe do rio. Tambem vivemos em Porto Amelia. Andamos por Nampula, Quelimane, Xai Xai, Inhambane, e Maputo. Agora estou nos Estados Unidos. I miss you, amigos e amigas, irmaos e irmas. Tenho saudades do meu paiz de infancia. Que Deus vos abencoe.

author: d8255d7f069a1b69f418b38cac0703596f649ea9f1363a13
body: Father, in the name of Jesus, we lift up Africa and the world to You and pray a hedge of protection around it. We thank You, Father that You are a wall of fire round about those that love You and that You set Your angels round about them. We seek the peace and welfare of Southern Africa. We pray to you for the cities and rural areas affected to have the aid needed for their citizens in need of food, housing, medical aid, shelter and counseling to recover and rebuild better places. Holy Spirit, we ask You to visit cities and open the eyes of the people, that they may turn from darkness to light and from the power of Satan to G-d, so that they may thus receive forgiveness and release from their sins and a place and portion among those who are consecrated and purified by faith in Jesus. Father, You see the regional and cultural strongholds that would hinder the Gospel. You have told us to declare Your works. So we say boldly that the prince of the power of the air, the god of this world who blinds the unbelievers' minds (that they should not discern the truth), is a defeated foe. We declare on the authority of Your Word that You have disarmed the powers and authorities; You made a public spectacle of them, triumphing over them by the Cross. Thank You, Father, for The Word and those that live by it. Amen

But in Mozambique, where already 1000 people have been found dead because of the consequences of the cyclone, people are waiting on trees for food and are homeless. body: Where is Mozambique?? Mozambique (/moʊzæmˈbiːk/), officially the Republic of Mozambique (Portuguese: Moçambique or República de Moçambique, pronounced [ʁɛˈpuβlikɐ ðɨ musɐ̃ˈbikɨ], Chichewa: Mozambiki, Swahili: Msumbiji, Tsonga: Muzambhiki), is a country located in Southeast Africa bordered by the Indian Ocean to the east, Tanzania to the north, Malawi and Zambia to the northwest, Zimbabwe to the west, and Eswatini (Swaziland) and South Africa to the southwest. The sovereign state is separated from the Comoros, Mayotte and Madagascar by the Mozambique Channel to the east. The capital of Mozambique is Maputo (formerly known as "Lourenço Marques" from 1876 to 1976) while Matola is the largest city, being a suburb of Maputo. Between the first and fifth centuries AD, Bantu-speaking peoples migrated to present-day Mozambique from farther north and west. Northern Mozambique lies within the monsoon trade winds of the Indian Ocean. Between the 7th and 11th centuries, a series of Swahili port towns developed here, which contributed to the development of a distinct Swahili culture and language. In the late medieval period, these towns were frequented by traders from Somalia, Ethiopia, Egypt, Arabia, Persia, and India.[9] The voyage of Vasco da Gama in 1498 marked the arrival of the Portuguese, who began a gradual process of colonisation and settlement in 1505. After over four centuries of Portuguese rule, Mozambique gained independence in 1975, becoming the People's Republic of Mozambique shortly thereafter. After only two years of independence, the country descended into an intense and protracted civil war lasting from 1977 to 1992. In 1994, Mozambique held its first multiparty elections, and has since remained a relatively stable presidential republic, although it still faces a low-intensity insurgency.[10] Mozambique is endowed with rich and extensive natural resources. The country's economy is based largely on agriculture, but industry is growing, mainly food and beverages, chemical manufacturing and aluminium and petroleum production. The tourism sector is also expanding. South Africa is Mozambique's main trading partner and source of foreign direct investment, while Belgium, Brazil, Portugal and Spain are also among the country's most important economic partners. Since 2001, Mozambique's annual average GDP growth has been among the world's highest. However, the country is still one of the poorest and most underdeveloped countries in the world,[11] ranking low in GDP per capita, human development, measures of inequality and average life expectancy. The only official language of Mozambique is Portuguese, which is spoken mostly as a second language by about half the population. Common native languages include Makhuwa, Sena, and Swahili. The country's population of around 29 million is composed overwhelmingly of Bantu people. The largest religion in Mozambique is Christianity, with significant minorities following Islam and African traditional religions. Mozambique is a member of the United Nations, the African Union, the Commonwealth of Nations, the Organisation of the Islamic Cooperation, the Community of Portuguese Language Countries, the Non-Aligned Movement and the Southern African Development Community, and is an observer at La Francophonie. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mozambique

author: e641237f354d8a455a95debd00a39e692ff574252b84d355
body: "Doadores governamentais e instituições internacionais (Lusofonia, CPLP, UE) e outras organizações acertem com o governo central e principalmente com a autarquia local - Conselho Municipal da Beira - o reencaminhamento do grosso dos donativos, com certeza para ajuda de emergência, mas acima de tudo, para contratar projectistas (Singapura) para desenhar um novo modelo de cidade inteligente para a nova cidade da Beira do século XXI" ... "Com 90% de destruição, pôr remendos de curto prazo não vai ajudar, e se é para gastar dinheiro (seja do Estado ou das Doações) que seja bem gasto, de preferência a pensar no enorme contributo que as novas tecnologias oferecem em áreas chave de planeamento urbano, como por exemplo, mobilidade, saúde, segurança e produtividade. e acima de tudo na prevenção das alterações climáticas que vão ser muito maiores no futuro. Isto é a base das cidades inteligentes." ... "como digo no meu post, esta medida requer envolvimento de projectistas urbanos de nova geração, formados e experimentados nas novas tecnologias enquanto criadores ou inovadores de metrópoles ou espaços urbanos. Esta é a ideia central, mas apesar disso seria necessário também que a gestão financeira das doações feitas para contrariar os efeitos do ciclone Idai, fosse feita de uma forma inteligente, a pensar, não só na ajuda de emergência, que com certeza é crucial neste momento, mas acima de tudo, a pensar no longo prazo, visto a cidade de Beira (de onde sou natural) ter sido construída há mais de cem anos atrás pelo colonizadores Portugueses, abaixo do nível do mar, sendo por isso agora, depois desta quase total devastação, uma oportunidade única para reconstruí-la inteligentemente."  https://sbr.com.sg/.../singapore-smartest-city-in-world... #beiraseculoxxi #beirarenascida @marinowriter @marinosyoga

author: 9ac7f77284ed466ae23a979fabe263ef1faa55caf468a6a5
body: @lenirasusana5693: _First of all africa's leaders are really corrupted_ I am aware of that. Ideally the constitutions of most African countries should be no more 2 terms of office. Obiang (Equatorial Guinea) has been in power 40 years, Museveni (Uganda) has been in power 33 years. Others are pretty lengthy. About the only leader I hold in regard is Nelson Mandela. Julius Nyerere of Tanzania and Jerry Rawlings of Ghana were relatively benign but each was 20 years. Botswana is relatively well run. But for corruption, you only have see the yearly Transparency International report and most African countries are near the bottom. And also reading the yearly Fragile States Index by Fund for Peace to gain an idea of how stable a country is. _And certainly hard for us youngsters trying to understand the sources of the problems and possible and practical solutions_ The sources? Well, I would say knowing why the West has arisen over 2700 years. And there is any one thing but a combination of reasons: Thales of Miletus in 624BC was the first person to start looking for Universal reasons to explain Particulars. Without him, there would be no science at all, no West. He was the first person to appreciate that the Greek Gods were myths. What Thales said was nonsense, but THE PROCESS he started wasn't nonsense. He effectively invented the scienctific method. That got lost during the Dark Ages (500AD to 1000AD) and was restarted in the Italian Renaissance from 1345AD onwards. Judeo-Christian Ethics, the rule of Law would be the second. The separation of Church and State comes from Jesus's teaching. Democracy would be third, again from Greeks, Socrates, Plato, Aristotle. Not a perfect system, but the least evil for governance. Worldview is a fourth. If you think the Universe is irrational, you wont achieve rationality. It will be beyond you. This cannot be overstated. It might be said, "Other nations played their part, like BrahmaGupta of India, discovering the properties of zero". To that, I would say, "Yes he was a genius, but where was his students? When he died, nothing continued. It is no good a nation having *JUST* one bright individual". During the Italian Renaissance and later Protestant Reformation, there were _MANY_ individuals at any given time, advancing knowledge in maths, physics, chemistry, biology, music, medicine, geology, engineering. Adam Smith's the Wealth of Nations, father of Capitalism would be fifth. Again flawed, not perfect but better than anything else. That has delivered sustained economic growth over many centuries. Basically if you follow the same principles, your nation will grow. And if you do not, it won't.

author: 7efe51ebac24cff1a92e26a598c21d845a37639f7afb601e
body: @barabbashaifamilitiaisrael8714 increasingly extreme weather events are consistent with the predictions of global warming. It is true that climate change has resulted in mass extinctions in the past, what seems strange is that knowing this the human race should be keen on triggering the next extinction event. All the science points to man made increases in green house gases will lead to a global temperature rise that will result in a major extension event and all the associated extreme weather and sea level rise that go with it. In other words, just because climate change happens naturally does not mean that human activity cannot also trigger it. It can be argued that as our sun ages and its output increases, the stability of the earth's temperature is eroded to the point where an emergent species such as humans can have an impact on the climate. As the negative feedback controls in the system move towards the positive, small changes have an ever increasing affect. Far from eroding climate stability, humans should be doing all that can to increase climate stability to delay as long as possible the inevitable natural changes that will result in a human extinction event.
